title: 14 Mobile Deposits
body_text:
14	Mobile Deposits
14.1	Overview of Mobile Deposits
Mobile Deposits are cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants that are deposited by customers to their accounts at your financial institution, using the Deposit Anywhere application on a mobile device. Payment items must be payable in Canadian dollars and drawn on a financial institution domiciled in Canada, or in the case of Receiver General Warrants, drawn on the Government of Canada.
To make a Mobile Deposit, a customer starts the application on a mobile device and takes a picture of the front and back of the payment item. CustomerГўВҖВҷs limits, as set by your financial institution, are verified and the quality of the image is checked.
If the deposit is approved, settlement to your customerГўВҖВҷs account can be posted immediately depending on your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs use of real-time messaging and settings for Mobile Deposits. Central 1 recommends that your financial institution hold funds on mobile deposits like it does on ATM items. The exact length of the hold is the responsibility of your financial institution.
Central 1 prepares Clearing Replacement Documents (CRDs) for items drawn on external financial institutions (i.e., financial institutions that arenГўВҖВҷt in BC, Ontario, or the Atlantic region). CRDs are sent through the clearing to the drawee branch instead of the physical items. If the cheque is drawn on a participating financial institution, itГўВҖВҷs exchanged between the financial institutions using images only.
Central 1 creates a deposit slip for every 200 mobile deposits. Authorized users can view images of mobile deposits, the automatically generated deposit slips, and deposit adjustment slips on ORS.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Mobile deposits are listed on the ADC ГўВҖВ“ Acknowledgement of Canadian Dollar Deposits report. Central 1 also provides the RDLC ГўВҖВ“ Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits report, which lists individual remote deposit items.
Central 1 provides a Mobile deposit file to each financial institution that participates in Mobile Deposits. If your financial institution participates in real-time messaging, the deposit file should be used to reconcile transactions communicated to the host banking system, since settlement to the customerГўВҖВҷs account is posted at the time of the deposit (although that is optional). If your financial institution doesnГўВҖВҷt use real-time messaging, the deposit file is imported into the host banking system and used to credit mobile deposits to customersГўВҖВҷ accounts automatically. This file is used for reconciliation and, if applicable, settlement purposes. See Section 14.6, Mobile Deposit File.
Central 1 facilitates settlement with the drawee branch.
Note:	Mobile Deposits are monitored in Central 1ГўВҖВҷs Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM). See the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
14.2	Enrolling in Mobile Deposits
Contact Central 1, Client Support Services, to enroll your financial institution in Mobile Deposits. Central 1 will work with your financial institution to implement the product with the appropriate settings and limits.
Your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Central 1 for the use of Mobile Deposits. Also, ensure that your customer signs an agreement with your financial institution for the use of this service before allowing them to initiate a Mobile Deposit. See Section 3.5, Agreements for Mobile Deposits, for more information.
14.3	Mobile Deposit Restrictions
The following restrictions apply to the use of Mobile Deposits:
Mobile deposits are available to personal and small business accounts only.
Your financial institution can restrict which customers have access to Mobile Deposits. Additionally, you can set a maximum deposit amount and a maximum number of deposits per day or per week by customer. Contact Central 1, Client Support Services, for information.
The deposit item must be a Canadian dollar cheque, draft, money order, or Receiver General Warrant.
The deposit item must be drawn on a financial institution domiciled in Canada (or, in the case of Receiver General Warrants, drawn on the Government of Canada).
If the deposit item is payable to multiple payees and the wording on the payment item indicates that itГўВҖВҷs payable to ГўВҖВңxxxx
and
xxxxГўВҖВқ, the payees must have a joint account at your financial institution. If the wording on the payment item indicates that itГўВҖВҷs payable to ГўВҖВңxxxx
and/or
xxxxГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңxxxx
or
xxxxГўВҖВқ, it doesnГўВҖВҷt need to be deposited to a joint account if the depositor is one of the payees named on the cheque.
14.4	Making a Mobile Deposit
To initiate a Mobile Deposit, a customer starts the application on a mobile device and takes a picture of the front and back of the payment item. The customerГўВҖВҷs limits, as set by your financial institution, are verified and the quality of the image is checked.
If the mobile application identifies a duplicate deposit, it wonГўВҖВҷt allow the deposit to proceed. This verification check applies only to deposits made through remote deposit capture channels (e.g., Mobile, Branch Capture Deposits). However, if a duplicate deposit is made at an ATM, the mobile application canГўВҖВҷt detect the other deposit. In this case, the drawee branch would dishonour and return the duplicate deposit to your financial institution within 90 days of clearing.
The customer must write the phrase ГўВҖВңdeposited via mobileГўВҖВқ on the cheque without obscuring any material information as soon as the deposit is accepted. This reduces the chance of the cheque being accidentally redeposited.
If your financial institution uses real-time messaging, the deposit may be settled to the customerГўВҖВҷs account immediately. If your financial institution doesnГўВҖВҷt use real-time messaging, you can use the Mobile Deposit File to automatically credit the customerГўВҖВҷs account. Alternatively, you can use the RDLC Report to manually credit customersГўВҖВҷ accounts. See Section 14.6, Mobile Deposit File, and Section 18.4, RDLC ГўВҖВ“ Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits. The Mobile Deposit File also assists you with reconciling Mobile Deposits. See Section 17.2, Settlement of Deposits to CustomerГўВҖВҷs Accounts, for more information.
It's the responsibility of your financial institution in conjunction with your customers to determine how and when the physical items are destroyed. CPA Rule A10, Image Rule, states that payment items deposited by image exchange must be destroyed within 120 days. See Section 4.9, Policy on Retention and Destruction of Items Processed via Remote Deposit Capture.
14.5	Processing Mobile Deposits at Central 1
Central 1 enters the deposit items into the clearing to obtain settlement from the drawee branch. These items are delivered to the appropriate data centres via the exchange of physical items (i.e., clearing replacement documents) or the exchange of image files.
Central 1 creates a deposit slip for every 200 mobile deposits. The amount recorded on the deposit slip is the sum total of the amounts that customers entered on the mobile banking application. If the amount a customer entered doesnГўВҖВҷt match the amount read by CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs cheque processing software, the deposit slip amount is out of balance and an adjustment is created. These discrepancies are identified on a best-efforts basis, using Central 1ГўВҖВҷs handwriting recognition software. Authorized users can view images of mobile deposits and the automatically generated deposit slips on ORS.
If a customer entered the deposit amount incorrectly, Central 1 prepares an adjustment slip. An image of the adjustment slip is available on ORS. Use the adjustment slip to correct the amount that was credited to the customerГўВҖВҷs account. See Section 17.3, Deposit Adjustments, for more information on deposit adjustments.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
For information on the settlement of mobile deposits to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Central 1 Account, see Section 17.1, Settlement and Reconciliation of Deposits to Your Financial InstitutionГўВҖВҷs Central 1 Account.
Mobile deposits are included in the following reports. See Chapter 18, Deposit Reports, for more information.
ADC ГўВҖВ“ Acknowledgement of Canadian Dollar Deposits
ГўВҖВ“ Lists the deposit slip total of both paper and mobile deposits.
RDLC ГўВҖВ“ Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits report
ГўВҖВ“ Lists individual mobile deposit transactions.
Authorized ORS users can view images of mobile deposits, the automatically generated deposit slips, and deposit adjustment slips on ORS. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on viewing these images.
14.6	Mobile Deposit File
Central 1 provides the Mobile Deposit File to financial institutions that participate in Mobile Deposits. If your financial institution uses real-time messaging, the Mobile Deposit File is used to reconcile transactions communicated to the host banking system.
If your financial institution doesnГўВҖВҷt use real-time messaging, you can automatically credit customersГўВҖВҷ accounts by importing the Mobile Deposit File to your host banking system. Alternatively, you can use the RDLC Report to manually credit customersГўВҖВҷ accounts. See Section 18.4, RDLC ГўВҖВ“ Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits. The Mobile Deposit File contains the depositing account number (i.e., your customerГўВҖВҷs account number), the transit number, and the amount of each cheque.
See the Mobile Deposit File Specifications for Financial Institutions, Form 3604, to view the deposit file specifications.
To obtain the deposit file, go to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange and download the RDCMmmdd.dat file from your root directory. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for procedures on accessing the File and Report Exchange. The file is in XML format and is generally available by 11 pm PT on the business day that Central 1 receives and processes the mobile deposits.
Related Information
Mobile Deposit File Specifications for Financial Institutions,
Form 3604
END
